---
title: "Tentative Title: Predictive capacity of oceanographic indices of recruitment"
subtitle: ""
author:
  - name: Megan L. Feddern
    email: 
    affiliation:
      - name: 
        department: 
  - name: Kristin Marshall
    email: 
    affiliation:
      - name: 
        department:
  - name: Nick Tolimieri
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    cite-method: citeproc
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
  docx: default
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: true
bibliography: references.bib
link-citations: true
csl: ecology.csl
citation:
  container-title: "Journal Name"
  volume: 1
  issue: 1
  doi: 10.1000/xyz123
abstract: |
  To be determined...
keywords: [cross-validation, recruitment, stock assessment]
---

```{r}
#| include: FALSE

library(knitr)
library(dplyr)
library(kableExtra)
library(tidyverse)
```
## General themes

- Balancing index stability versus predictive capacity. Generally, both are important for indices in stock assessment application. Due to the ephemeral nature of many indices of recruitment, and the challenges of changing ocean dynamics (nonstationarity, extremes on threshold or dome shaped relationships), its important that indices used to inform management decisions are both robust and can support predictions. 

-

## If we only fit models from 1993 - 2010 do the inconsistencies in selection criteria change?

### Methods

We repeated the same methods from the March analysis but for all species the models were only fit from 1993 - 2010. Covariates were re-standardized over teh 1993 - 2010 period. We also omitted the rolling window analysis (as only two 15-year windows could be used with the reduced dataset).

### Findings

**This is not more consistent BUT the problem seems to shift. The issue here seems to be time series length rather than spanning a regime (training on 7 years of data and predicting 10 or training on 13 years of data and predicting 5 simply does not work well)**

Overall, leave-future out criteria still performs poorly when applied to the 1993 - 2010 time period but this poor performance seems to be driven by short time series length rather than spanning seemingly distinct time periods. Additionally, the variables that are unstable from 1993 - 2018 tend to also be unstable over the 1993 - 2010 period. There seems to be two factors that jeopardize the utility of LFO in these circumstances. The first is whether or not there is an apparent regime shift in the oceanographic data and the second is time series length. Regardless of these two circumstances, variables that are unstable for a short time window without a regime shift seem to also be unstable in longer time series when a regime shift is included. Much like including the full time series, the best models, and the variables included in the best models, vary by selection criteria [@tbl-ps] - [@tbl-hk]

![Marginal mean improvement of RMSE for each oceanographic driver and each selecetion criteria (leave-future-out last 10 years, LFO10; leave-future-out last 5 years, LFO5; leave-one-out all years)](Output/Figures/ShortenedTS/MarginalMeanRMSE.png){#fig-rmse}

![Mohn's rho (right) and assosciated funtional relationships(left) for each of the 8 "peeled" years for each species. Mohn's rho can be interpreted as deviations from 0 indicate functional relationships that differ from the model fit to the full 1994 - 2010 time period](Output/Figures/ShortenedTS/all_best_loo.png){#fig-fr}

![Absolute value of Mohn's rho for each oceanographic predictor for each of the 8 "peeled" years for each species. Mohn's rho can be interpreted as deviations from 0 indicate functional relationships that differ from the model fit to the full 1994 - 2010 time period](Output/Figures/ShortenedTS/mohns_single_plot.png){#fig-mohns}

![Mean Mohns rho by terminal year for single predictor models showing depicting mean values across oceanographic predictors by species with a loess smooth by species](Output/Figures/ShortenedTS/mohnstrend.png){#fig-mohnstrend}
{{< pagebreak >}}
::: {.landscape}
```{r}
#| label: tbl-ps
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for petrale sole using each of the three selection criteria. Leave-future-out could not differentiate between 100 competing models"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

ps<-read.csv("Output/Data/ShortenedTS/ps_table.csv")
loops<-ps%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5ps<-ps%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10ps<-ps%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

ps_table<-loops%>%bind_rows(lfo5ps)%>%bind_rows(lfo10ps)
ps_table[, sapply(ps_table, is.numeric)] <- round(ps_table[, sapply(ps_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

kable(ps_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(ps_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 


```
{{< pagebreak >}}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-sb_combined
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for sablefish using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

sb<-read.csv("Output/Data/ShortenedTS/sb_table.csv")
loosb<-sb%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5sb<-sb%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10sb<-sb%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

#nrow(sb%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(sb$RMSE10))))
#nrow(sb%>%filter(RMSE5==1.05))

sb_table<-loosb%>%bind_rows(lfo5sb)%>%bind_rows(lfo10sb)
sb_table[, sapply(sb_table, is.numeric)] <- round(sb_table[, sapply(sb_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

kable(sb_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(sb_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```
{{< pagebreak >}}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-yt
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for yellowtail using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

yt<-read.csv("Output/Data/ShortenedTS/yt_table.csv")
looyt<-yt%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5yt<-yt%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10yt<-yt%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

yt_table<-looyt%>%bind_rows(lfo5yt)%>%bind_rows(lfo10yt)
yt_table[, sapply(yt_table, is.numeric)] <- round(yt_table[, sapply(yt_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

#nrow(yt%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(yt$RMSE10))))
#nrow(yt%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(yt$RMSE5))))

kable(yt_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(yt_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```



{{< pagebreak >}}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-hk
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for hake using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

hk<-read.csv("Output/Data/ShortenedTS/hk_table.csv")
loohk<-hk%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5hk<-hk%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10hk<-hk%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

hk_table<-loohk%>%bind_rows(lfo5hk)%>%bind_rows(lfo10hk)
hk_table[, sapply(hk_table, is.numeric)] <- round(hk_table[, sapply(hk_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

nrow(hk%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(hk$RMSE10))))
nrow(hk%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(hk$RMSE5))))

kable(hk_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(hk_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```
:::
{{< pagebreak >}}

## If we are more restrictive in the correlation we allow across oceanographic variables, are our results more consistent across ways of expressing predictive capacity?

### Methods

We repeated the same methods from the March analysis but for all species the models only used combinations of variables that had a pearson's correlation coefficient of 0.3 or less to reduce the number of covariates. 

### Findings

**Overall this does not help. The issues we are encountering is not because of correlations across time series, at least not when models are restricted to only 3 covariates and a correlation coefficient of 0.5**

As expected, reducing the correlation coefficient has no impact on the highest ranked variables based on marginal mean improvement of RMSE and nor does it impact the ranking of stability. This is expected because of the nature of the ways these variables are calculated (either using models for only one variable based on stability or by reducing the impact of redundant variables in the case of marginal mean improvement RMSE).

But, we do see subtle differences in the rolling window analysis and the highest ranked models. Overall, this seems to be an ineffective way to minimize issues with predictive capacity indicating that overfitting or redundancy in oceanographic time series does not seem to be the primary issue. 

![Marginal mean improvement of each oceanographic driver useing 15-year time windows for each of the three groundfish species of interest](Output/Figures/CorrelationReduced/rollingplot.png){#fig-rw}
{{< pagebreak >}}
::: {.landscape}
```{r}
#| label: tbl-ps2
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for petrale sole using each of the three selection criteria. Leave-future-out could not differentiate between 100 competing models"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

ps<-read.csv("Output/Data/CorrelationReduced/ps_table.csv")
loops<-ps%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5ps<-ps%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10ps<-ps%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

ps_table<-loops%>%bind_rows(lfo5ps)%>%bind_rows(lfo10ps)
ps_table[, sapply(ps_table, is.numeric)] <- round(ps_table[, sapply(ps_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

kable(ps_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(ps_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 


```
{{< pagebreak >}}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-sb2
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for sablefish using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

sb<-read.csv("Output/Data/CorrelationReduced/sb_table.csv")
loosb<-sb%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5sb<-sb%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10sb<-sb%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

#nrow(sb%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(sb$RMSE10))))
#nrow(sb%>%filter(RMSE5==1.05))

sb_table<-loosb%>%bind_rows(lfo5sb)%>%bind_rows(lfo10sb)
sb_table[, sapply(sb_table, is.numeric)] <- round(sb_table[, sapply(sb_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

kable(sb_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(sb_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```
{{< pagebreak >}}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-yt2
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for yellowtail using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

yt<-read.csv("Output/Data/CorrelationReduced/yt_table.csv")
looyt<-yt%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5yt<-yt%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10yt<-yt%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

yt_table<-looyt%>%bind_rows(lfo5yt)%>%bind_rows(lfo10yt)
yt_table[, sapply(yt_table, is.numeric)] <- round(yt_table[, sapply(yt_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

#nrow(yt%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(yt$RMSE10))))
#nrow(yt%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(yt$RMSE5))))

kable(yt_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(yt_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```



{{< pagebreak >}}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-hk2
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for hake using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

hk<-read.csv("Output/Data/CorrelationReduced/hk_table.csv")
loohk<-hk%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_loo)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LOO")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo5hk<-hk%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE5)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO5")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)
lfo10hk<-hk%>%
  select(variables, Hit, dev.ex, AIC, RMSE_loo,RMSE5, RMSE10)%>%
  arrange(RMSE10)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="LFO10")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)

hk_table<-loohk%>%bind_rows(lfo5hk)%>%bind_rows(lfo10hk)
hk_table[, sapply(hk_table, is.numeric)] <- round(hk_table[, sapply(hk_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

nrow(hk%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(hk$RMSE10))))
nrow(hk%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(hk$RMSE5))))

kable(hk_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(hk_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```
:::

{{< pagebreak >}}

## If we remove "unstable" variables, is predictive capacity more consistent across variables?

### Methods
To do this analysis we fit the analysis approach we used in March (full time series 1994 - 2018; correlation coefficient of 0.5) and removed all variables that had a Mohn's rho of 0.1 (see @fig-mohns for reference). This reduced the number of variables used for petrale sole by 7, for hake by 9, for yellowtail by 4, and for sablefish by 8.

### Findings

**Overall this does seem to produce clearer "winners" and "losers" than using all of the variables but selection criteria are still not consistent (with the exception of hake). There may be certain "types" variables that are inherently less stable than others (i.e., degree days)**

Interestingly, yellowtail had the most stable best model in all analyses but also had the least number of unstable predictors. We hypothesiszed that this may be due to yellowtail having basin-scale time series (ONI and CUTI) as covariates but it also seems that overall there was just more stability across all of the time series. One other possible mechanism here is that yellowtail seems to have the smallest spatial range over which these covariates are summarized (exclusively within the NCC). Maybe characterizing them over such large spatial ranges contributes to instability?

When unstable variables are excluded, it becomes much more obvious which variables both provide stability (highly ranked in marginal mean improvement RMSE for LOO) and which also provide improved predictive capacity on 5 and 10 year time horizons (MMI RMSE for leave-future-out). Of the 4 species, there is at least 1 highly ranked LOO covariate that also provides improved predictive capacity (@fig-rmseunstable). 

There are also some general patterns of which types of variables that were considered for more than 1 species are unstable. For example, degree day variables are unstable 40% of the time when considered as a covariate of recruitment. **(NOTE TO SELF add in basin-scale indices once I add them to hake, consider months of year?)**

![Marginal mean improvement of RMSE for each oceanographic driver and each selecetion criteria (leave-future-out last 10 years, LFO10; leave-future-out last 5 years, LFO5; leave-one-out all years)](Output/Figures/UnstableRemoved/MarginalMeanRMSE.png){#fig-rmseunstable}

![For types of covariates considered for more than one species, the total number (n) of covariates that were considered unstable and the rate (number unstable / total number considered)](Output/Figures/UnstableRemoved/countrate.png){#fig-countrate}

## If we used a "combined" selection criteria or "ensembled" best models, what do the predictions look like?
### Methods
To consider which indicators had the best predictive capacity overall across metrics, marginal mean improvement was averaged across the 3 selection criteria.

To conduct this analysis, I took the results for the March analysis (1994 - 2018; correlation = 0.5). For every model, RMSE LOO and RMSE LFO 5-year were combined (summed) and RMSE LOO was given a weight of 2 to prduce a "combined" RMSE. The model with the lowest combined RMSE was ranked as the best performng model. We then compared the 1-year ahead prediction of the last 5 years of this model to the best model with the other 3 selection criteria (LOO, LFO-5, LFO-10).

To consider an ensemble model, the predictions for 1-year ahead of the top 5 models for each of the 3 selection criteria (LOO, LFO-5, LFO-10) were averaged. No weighting was done.

### Findings

- Combining selection criteria is informative for understanding which variables balance both predictive capacity and stability (@fig-marg_all)
- In all circumstances, the best model using a combined selection criteria was one of the other highly ranked models, and helps dinstinguish between models with very similar RMSE for LFO (@fig-modeltscomb)
- An ensemble approach isn't really helpful, most interannual variability is washed out ()

![One year ahead predictions of recrutiment deviations (black line) for the last ten years (A., B., C.) and and leave-one-out predictions for all years (B., D., F.) for the best supported models based on RMSE for one year ahead predictions for the last 10 years (LFO10, blue), last 5 years (LFO5, yellow), RMSE for leave-one-out predictions (red, LOO) and combined selection criteria (pink). Rows depict individual species, Petrale Sole (A.,B.), Sablefish (C. D.) and Yellowtail reockfish (E. F.)](Output/Figures/Combined/all_ts.png){#fig-modeltscomb}

![One-year-ahead predictions for the ensembled models](Output/Figures/Combined/all_ensemble.png){#fig-marg_all}

![Marginal mean improvement of RMSE for each oceanographic driver across selection criteria (leave-future-out last 10 years, LFO10; leave-future-out last 5 years, LFO5; leave-one-out all years)](Output/Figures/Combined/marg_all_plot.png){#fig-marg_all}

```{r}
#| label: tbl-yt3
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for yellowtail using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

yt<-read.csv("Output/Data/Combined/yt_table.csv")
combinedyt<-yt%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC,RMSE_combined)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_combined)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="Combined")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)


yt_table<-combinedyt
yt_table[, sapply(yt_table, is.numeric)] <- round(yt_table[, sapply(yt_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

#nrow(yt%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(yt$RMSE10))))
#nrow(yt%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(yt$RMSE5))))

kable(yt_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(yt_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-ps3
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for petrale sole using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

ps<-read.csv("Output/Data/Combined/ps_table.csv")
combinedps<-ps%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC,RMSE_combined)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_combined)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="Combined")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)


ps_table<-combinedps
ps_table[, sapply(ps_table, is.numeric)] <- round(ps_table[, sapply(ps_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

#nrow(ps%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(ps$RMSE10))))
#nrow(ps%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(ps$RMSE5))))

kable(ps_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(ps_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-sb3
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for sablefish using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

sb<-read.csv("Output/Data/Combined/sb_table.csv")
combinedsb<-sb%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC,RMSE_combined)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_combined)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="Combined")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)


sb_table<-combinedsb
sb_table[, sapply(sb_table, is.numeric)] <- round(sb_table[, sapply(sb_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

#nrow(sb%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(sb$RMSE10))))
#nrow(sb%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(sb$RMSE5))))

kable(sb_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(sb_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```

```{r}
#| label: tbl-hk3
#| tbl-cap: "Top 5 models for hake using each of the three selection criteria"
#| output: asis
#| echo: FALSE
#| message: FALSE
#| warning: FALSE

hk<-read.csv("Output/Data/Combined/hk_table.csv")
combinedhk<-hk%>%
  select(variables,Hit,  dev.ex, AIC,RMSE_combined)%>%
  arrange(RMSE_combined)%>%
  mutate(SelectionCriteria="Combined")%>%
  slice_head(n = 5)


hk_table<-combinedhk
hk_table[, sapply(hk_table, is.numeric)] <- round(hk_table[, sapply(hk_table, is.numeric)], digits = 2)

#nrow(hk%>%filter(RMSE10==min(na.omit(hk$RMSE10))))
#nrow(hk%>%filter(RMSE5==min(na.omit(hk$RMSE5))))

kable(hk_table)%>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), 
                full_width = FALSE)%>%
    kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover")) %>%
  # Use pack_rows to create the subheadings based on the 'Group' column
  # Remove the 'Group' column from the main table display with remove_column
  pack_rows(
    index = table(fct_inorder(hk_table$SelectionCriteria)), # Automatically determine where groups start/end
  # remove_column = TRUE, # Hide the 'Group' column in the final table
    bold = TRUE, # Make subheadings bold
    background = "#f7f7f7" # Optional: add a light background color
  ) %>%
  column_spec(column = 1, width = "5cm") 
```
## Are basin scale variables more stable than these local variables?
### Methods
Procure data from KM and NT
Add Hake and sablefish variables as listed in the notes


# TO DO

## Does seasonality impact stability?

## Are there patterns in the relative importance of variables through time?

### Methods
DFA on rolling window
